name: The Hidden Confession Artifact
description: A mundane task, such as sorting through a dead man's belongings, is derailed by the discovery of a secret journal or letter.

name: Name-on-the-Page Shock
description: The protagonist is reading the artifact and suddenly sees their own name, which re-centers the mystery on them personally.

name: Posthumous Mentor Reframe
description: The deceased authority figure is re-characterized through their private writing, often revealing regret or fear regarding the protagonist.

name: The Interrupted Sentence
description: The confession or letter cuts off mid-thought or mid-sentence.

name: The Mourner’s Inventory
description: The character catalogues the mundane relics of the dead (sorting belongings), which serves as a backdrop for grief.

name: Haunted Architecture as Witness
description: The setting itself (room, fortress) is treated as a sentient entity that observes the character's private grief.

name: The Symbolic Object Drop
description: The character physically drops the journal or artifact and refuses to retrieve it immediately.

name: Environmental Pathetic Fallacy
description: The outside weather, lighting, or setting noise perfectly reflects the character's internal crisis.
